name: raven-r
description: R language server with cross-file awareness, plus an R console and plot, data, and help viewers

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Raven: Knit Preview executes R code from the workspace and is disabled in untrusted workspaces. Executable-path settings are also read from user/global settings only.

commands.title: Terminal - Run Current Chunk and Move

keybindings.when: editorTextFocus && (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled
editorTextFocus && raven.rConsoleEnabled && raven.rmdKnit.enabled && (editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == r) && resourceExtname =~ /\.(rmd|Rmd|RMD)$/
editorTextFocus && (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled
editorTextFocus && (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled
editorTextFocus && (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled
editorTextFocus && (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled
editorTextFocus && (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled

editor/title.when: (editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled
(editorLangId == r || editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto) && raven.rConsoleEnabled && raven.isRPackage

editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto || (editorLangId == r && resourceExtname =~ /\.(rmd|Rmd|RMD|qmd|Qmd|QMD)$/)
editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto || (editorLangId == r && resourceExtname =~ /\.(rmd|Rmd|RMD|qmd|Qmd|QMD)$/)
editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto || (editorLangId == r && resourceExtname =~ /\.(rmd|Rmd|RMD|qmd|Qmd|QMD)$/)
editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto || (editorLangId == r && resourceExtname =~ /\.(rmd|Rmd|RMD|qmd|Qmd|QMD)$/)

editorLangId == rmd || editorLangId == quarto || (editorLangId == r && resourceExtname =~ /\.(rmd|Rmd|RMD|qmd|Qmd|QMD)$/)

colors.description: Background color for runnable R code chunks in .Rmd / .qmd files and `# %%` cells in .R files.
Background color for R code chunks marked `eval = FALSE` (or `eval = F`). Should be subtler than the active background.
Top border color drawn above the active `# %%` cell in .R cell mode. Mirrors VS Code's interactive-cell selected-cell indicator.
Bottom border color drawn below the active `# %%` cell in .R cell mode.

raven.rTerminal.program.description: Program to use for the R terminal. 'R' is the standard R console; 'arf' and 'radian' are modern third-party R consoles (must be installed separately). If the configured program is not found in your shell PATH, Raven prompts you to switch to 'R' on this machine, leaving your User-level default unchanged.

raven.sendToR.advanceCursorOnSend.description: Advance cursor to the next line after sending a single statement to R.

enumDescriptions: Paste short blocks directly; write blocks with ≥ raven.sendToR.autoTempFileThresholdLines lines to a temp file and run with source().
Paste code directly. Multi-line blocks use bracketed paste mode.
Write code to a temporary file and run source() for both single-line and multi-line sends.
raven.sendToR.sendMethod.description: Controls how Raven sends code to R. 'auto' pastes short blocks directly and switches to a temp file once a block has at least raven.sendToR.autoTempFileThresholdLines lines (≥ N). 'paste' always pastes, using bracketed paste mode for multi-line code. 'tempfile' always writes to a temp file and runs source().

raven.sendToR.autoTempFileThresholdLines.markdownDescription: Line-count threshold at which `auto` send mode switches from pasting to writing the code to a temporary file. A block with **≥ N lines** is written to a temp file and run with `source()`; a block with fewer than N lines is pasted directly. So `25` (the default) means: paste up to 24 lines, temp-file 25 or more. Set to `2` to reproduce the prior behavior of always temp-filing any multi-line block. Lower it on slow remote connections (SSH, VS Code Remote) where large pastes can be garbled; raise it if you prefer to see your code echoed in the terminal. Has no effect when `sendMethod` is `paste` or `tempfile`.

raven.server.path.description: Path to raven binary. If empty, uses bundled binary.

raven.trace.server.description: Trace level for the Raven language server. 'messages' shows debug output, 'verbose' shows full trace output including diagnostic decisions.

raven.editor.dotInWordSeparators.description: Whether to exclude dots from word separators in R and JAGS files. 'ask' prompts on first use, 'yes' always applies the setting, 'no' never applies it.

raven.crossFile.maxBackwardDepth.description: Maximum depth for backward traversal when resolving cross-file dependencies. Controls how many levels of parent files (files that source the current file) are analyzed. Higher values provide more complete analysis but may impact performance in deeply nested projects.

raven.crossFile.maxForwardDepth.description: Maximum depth for forward traversal when resolving cross-file dependencies. Controls how many levels of sourced files (files sourced by the current file) are analyzed. Higher values provide more complete symbol resolution but may impact performance.

raven.crossFile.maxChainDepth.description: Maximum total chain depth for cross-file dependency resolution. Limits the combined depth of forward and backward traversal to prevent excessive analysis in complex dependency graphs. Default is 20.

enumDescriptions: Assume symbols from sourced files are available from the start of the file
Assume symbols from sourced files are available from the end of the file
raven.crossFile.assumeCallSite.description: Default call site assumption when the actual source() call location cannot be determined. Controls symbol availability: 'start' makes symbols available from the beginning of the file, 'end' makes them available only after the presumed source() call at the end.

enumDescriptions: Infer parent files by scanning the workspace for source() calls and forward directives. Diagnostics are deferred until the workspace scan completes.
Only use explicit @lsp-sourced-by directives to resolve parent files. Diagnostics are shown immediately without waiting for a workspace scan.
raven.crossFile.backwardDependencies.description: Controls how Raven discovers files that source the current file. 'auto' scans the workspace, and 'explicit' requires @lsp-sourced-by directives.

raven.crossFile.indexWorkspace.description: Enable workspace indexing for cross-file awareness. When enabled, closed files in the workspace are indexed to provide symbol information for cross-file features like completions and go-to-definition.

raven.crossFile.maxRevalidationsPerTrigg.description: Maximum number of dependent files to revalidate when a file changes. Limits the cascade of diagnostic updates to prevent performance issues in projects with many cross-file dependencies.

raven.crossFile.revalidationDebounceMs.description: Debounce delay in milliseconds before triggering cross-file revalidation after a file change. Higher values reduce CPU usage during rapid editing but delay diagnostic updates.

enumDescriptions: Report missing sourced files as errors
Report missing sourced files as warnings
Report missing sourced files as information
Report missing sourced files as hints
raven.crossFile.missingFileSeverity.description: Severity level for diagnostics when a sourced file cannot be found. Controls how missing file references in source() calls and LSP directives are reported.

raven.crossFile.circularDependencySeveri.description: Severity level for diagnostics when circular dependencies are detected between files. Circular dependencies occur when file A sources file B which directly or indirectly sources file A.

enumDescriptions: Report out-of-scope symbol usage as errors
Report out-of-scope symbol usage as warnings
Report out-of-scope symbol usage as information
Report out-of-scope symbol usage as hints
raven.crossFile.outOfScopeSeverity.description: Severity level for diagnostics when symbols are used before they are available in scope. This can occur when referencing symbols from a sourced file before the source() call.

enumDescriptions: Report max chain depth exceeded as errors
Report max chain depth exceeded as warnings
Report max chain depth exceeded as information
Report max chain depth exceeded as hints
raven.crossFile.maxChainDepthSeverity.description: Severity level for diagnostics when the maximum chain depth is exceeded during cross-file dependency resolution. This indicates the dependency chain is deeper than the configured maxChainDepth limit.

raven.crossFile.redundantDirectiveSeveri.description: Severity level for diagnostics when an @lsp-source directive (without line=) targets the same file as an earlier source() call. The directive is redundant since the source() call already makes the symbols available.

raven.crossFile.onDemandIndexing.enabled.description: Enable on-demand background indexing of files not currently open in the editor. When enabled, files referenced by source() calls and LSP directives are automatically indexed to provide cross-file symbol resolution.

raven.crossFile.onDemandIndexing.maxTran.description: Maximum depth for transitive dependency indexing. Controls how many levels of indirect dependencies (files sourced by sourced files) are indexed in the background. Higher values provide more complete cross-file awareness but increase indexing workload.

raven.crossFile.onDemandIndexing.maxQueu.description: Maximum number of files that can be queued for background indexing. Limits memory usage and prevents excessive background work in projects with many transitive dependencies.

raven.crossFile.cache.metadataMaxEntries.description: Maximum entries in the metadata cache. Uses LRU eviction when full.

raven.crossFile.cache.fileContentMaxEntr.description: Maximum file contents cached for cross-file resolution. Each entry stores full file text. Uses LRU eviction when full.

raven.crossFile.cache.existenceMaxEntrie.description: Maximum file existence check results cached. These are small (path + boolean). Uses LRU eviction when full.

raven.crossFile.cache.workspaceIndexMaxE.description: Maximum closed files in the cross-file workspace index. Each entry includes parsed metadata and scope artifacts. Uses LRU eviction when full.

raven.diagnostics.enabled.description: Enable or disable all diagnostics. When disabled, no diagnostic messages will be shown regardless of individual severity settings.

raven.diagnostics.undefinedVariableSever.description: Severity level for diagnostics when a variable is used but not defined in the current scope, sourced files, or loaded packages. Set to "off" to suppress undefined variable diagnostics if they produce too many false positives in your workflow.

enumDescriptions: Report mixed `&`/`|` without parentheses as errors
Report mixed `&`/`|` without parentheses as warnings
Report mixed `&`/`|` without parentheses as information
Report mixed `&`/`|` without parentheses as hints
raven.diagnostics.mixedLogicalSeverity.description: Severity for the mixed-logical diagnostic. Flags `|`/`||` whose immediate operand is a bare `&`/`&&` (without explicit parentheses), e.g. `a & b | c`. Because `&` binds more tightly than `|` in R, the grouping is easy to mis-read. Stops at call/subset boundaries so vectorized data-mask patterns are not flagged.

raven.diagnostics.conditionAssignmentSev.description: Severity for the condition-assignment diagnostic. Flags `=` used as a binary operator directly inside an `if` or `while` condition. R rejects `if (x = 1)` as a syntax error at runtime but tree-sitter-r accepts it silently. Named-argument `=` inside function calls within the condition is not flagged.

raven.packages.enabled.description: Enable package awareness features. When enabled, Raven analyzes library() calls to provide completions, hover information, and diagnostics for R package functions. Disable this setting if you don't need package-aware features or want to reduce resource usage.

raven.packages.additionalLibraryPaths.description: Additional library paths to search for R packages. These paths are searched in addition to the default R library paths. Useful for projects with packages installed in non-standard locations or custom library directories.

raven.packages.rPath.description: Path to the R executable used for non-interactive subprocess calls (package introspection, library path discovery). If empty, Raven searches for R in the system PATH. Specify this setting if R is installed in a non-standard location or if you want to use a specific R installation. Must point to vanilla R, not radian or arf — those are interactive REPL wrappers and cannot run the non-interactive scripts Raven uses. For the interactive terminal program, see raven.rTerminal.program.

raven.packages.missingPackageSeverity.description: Severity level for diagnostics when a package referenced in library() cannot be found. Controls how missing package references are reported in the editor.

raven.packages.watchLibraryPaths.description: Watch R library paths for package install/remove events and refresh diagnostics automatically. Disable if you see excessive filesystem activity or inotify watch-limit errors on Linux.

raven.packages.watchDebounceMs.description: Debounce window for batching library path filesystem events, in milliseconds. Larger values reduce redundant work during bulk installs (e.g. renv::restore); smaller values respond faster.

enumDescriptions: Detect automatically: enable package mode when a DESCRIPTION file is found at the workspace root.
Always enable package mode (for non-standard package layouts without DESCRIPTION).
Never enable package mode, even if DESCRIPTION exists.
raven.packages.packageMode.description: Controls R package workspace mode. When active, all R/*.R files are treated as mutually visible (flat namespace), and imports from NAMESPACE/roxygen suppress undefined-variable diagnostics.

raven.symbols.workspaceMaxResults.description: Maximum number of symbols returned by workspace symbol search (Ctrl+T / Cmd+T). Higher values show more results but may impact performance. Valid range: 100-10000.

raven.completion.triggerOnOpenParen.description: Trigger parameter completions when typing '(' after a function name. When disabled, parameter suggestions require manual invocation (Ctrl+Space).

enumDescriptions: Aligns same-line arguments to opening paren (RStudio convention)
Indents all arguments relative to previous line
Disables AST-aware indentation (Tier 2), keeping only basic declarative rules (Tier 1)
raven.indentation.style.description: Indentation style for R code. Controls the AST-aware (Tier 2) indentation behavior. Set to 'off' to disable Tier 2 while keeping basic indentation rules active.

enumDescriptions: Always activate Raven's R console, plot viewer, and data viewer.
Never activate Raven's R console, plot viewer, or data viewer.
Activate Raven's R console, plot viewer, and data viewer unless the REditorSupport (R) extension is enabled or VS Code is running as Positron.
raven.rConsole.activation.description: Controls when Raven activates its R-language IDE surfaces: the R console, plot/data viewers, chunk run commands, and the .Rmd knit command. The default `auto` resolves to `disabled` when the REditorSupport (R) extension is enabled or VS Code is running as Positron, so Raven doesn't duplicate surfaces those provide. Code intelligence and the help viewer are unaffected by this setting. See https://github.com/jbearak/raven/blob/main/docs/coexistence.md.

enumDescriptions: Use the directory containing the .Rmd file (default).
Use the workspace folder containing the .Rmd file.
Use R's startup working directory (don't pass knit_root_dir).
raven.knit.workingDirectory.description: Working directory passed to rmarkdown::render's knit_root_dir argument. Matches RStudio's Knit Directory submenu.

raven.knit.timeoutMs.description: Hard timeout (milliseconds) for the Raven: Knit Preview subprocess. After this elapses, Raven escalates the kill ladder (SIGINT → SIGTERM → SIGKILL) and surfaces a 'Knit timed out' message.

raven.knit.export.timeoutMs.description: Timeout (milliseconds) for the Pandoc subprocess used by Knit: Export to PDF / Word / HTML. Pandoc itself is fast; this bounds runaway invocations.

raven.pandoc.path.description: Absolute path to a Pandoc binary. Leave empty to use PATH and standard install locations (Homebrew, /usr/local/bin, RStudio's bundled pandoc, etc.).

raven.pandoc.pdfEngine.description: PDF engine used by Pandoc when exporting to PDF. Most users should leave this at xelatex; tectonic is a fully self-contained LaTeX alternative, and wkhtmltopdf renders via HTML/WebKit instead of LaTeX.

raven.knit.fontFamily.patternErrorMessage: Cannot contain ; { } < > \ or whitespace control characters.
raven.knit.fontFamily.description: Body/prose font for the Raven: Knit Preview output. Accepts the same comma-separated form as CSS `font-family` (quoted names with spaces or parens are fine — e.g. `"Aptos (Body)", sans-serif`). Leave empty to inherit `markdown.preview.fontFamily`. The open preview panel updates live when this setting changes; the rendered `.html` on disk refreshes on the next knit. Bare parens, unbalanced quotes, and CSS-wide keywords (`inherit`, `initial`, …) are rejected at knit time and the next layer of the fallback chain is used.

raven.knit.monospaceFontFamily.patternErrorMessage: Cannot contain ; { } < > \ or whitespace control characters.
raven.knit.monospaceFontFamily.description: Monospace font for both highlighted code chunks and bare output blocks in the Raven: Knit Preview output. Accepts the same comma-separated form as CSS `font-family` (quoted names with spaces or parens are fine). Leave empty to inherit `editor.fontFamily` (honors `[rmd]` / `[quarto]` language-scoped overrides). The open preview panel updates live when this setting changes; the rendered `.html` on disk refreshes on the next knit. Bare parens, unbalanced quotes, and CSS-wide keywords are rejected at knit time and the next layer of the fallback chain is used.

enumDescriptions: Open the plot viewer in the active editor column.
Open the plot viewer beside the active editor.
raven.plot.viewerColumn.description: Initial editor column when the plot viewer first opens. Once you move the panel, Raven leaves it where you put it.

enumDescriptions: Open the help viewer in the active editor column.
Open the help viewer beside the active editor.
raven.help.viewerColumn.description: Initial editor column when the R help viewer first opens. Once you move the panel, Raven leaves it where you put it.

enumDescriptions: Color the cell text for NA / NaN.
Tint the cell background for NA / NaN.
Render missing values like any other cell.
raven.dataViewer.missingValueStyle.description: How the data viewer highlights NA / NaN cells.

raven.dataViewer.maxStoredLayouts.description: Maximum number of persisted column-layout entries (per panel name × schema hash) before LRU eviction.

raven.dataViewer.defaultDigits.description: Initial digits used by the data viewer's Format toggle.

raven.linting.enabled.description: Controls native lint diagnostics. "auto" (default) enables linting when a .lintr or raven.toml opts in. true/"on" forces linting on. false/"off" disables linting unless a discovered raven.toml explicitly sets enabled = true (it always overrides .lintr auto-discovery). See docs/linting.md for the full resolution matrix.

raven.linting.lineLength.description: Maximum allowed line length for the line-length lint. Width is measured in UTF-16 code units. Set raven.linting.lineLengthSeverity to "off" to disable the rule without affecting other lints.

enumDescriptions: Require `<-` for assignment; flag top-level `=` assignments.
Require `=` for assignment; flag top-level `<-` assignments.
raven.linting.assignmentOperator.description: Preferred assignment operator. The lint never flags `=` inside argument lists (named arguments).

raven.linting.lineLengthSeverity.description: Severity for the line-length lint. Set to "off" to disable the rule.

raven.linting.trailingWhitespaceSeverity.description: Severity for trailing whitespace at end of line. Set to "off" to disable the rule.

raven.linting.noTabSeverity.description: Severity for tab characters in source. Set to "off" to disable the rule.

enumDescriptions: Report trailing blank lines as errors
Report trailing blank lines as warnings
Report trailing blank lines as information
Report trailing blank lines as hints
raven.linting.trailingBlankLinesSeverity.description: Severity for trailing blank lines and missing final newline at end of file. Set to "off" to disable the rule.

enumDescriptions: Report mismatched assignment operator as errors
Report mismatched assignment operator as warnings
Report mismatched assignment operator as information
Report mismatched assignment operator as hints
raven.linting.assignmentOperatorSeverity.description: Severity for the assignment-operator lint. Set to "off" to disable the rule.

enumDescriptions: Require lowercase words separated by underscores (e.g. my_function)
Require lowercase first letter, capitalized subsequent words (e.g. myFunction)
Require lowercase words separated by dots (e.g. my.function)
Require uppercase words separated by underscores (e.g. MY_CONSTANT)
Require a single all-lowercase word (e.g. mything)
Disable the function-name check (variable and argument checks may still run)
raven.linting.objectNameStyleFunction.description: Required naming scheme for functions (assignments whose value is a function definition). Names that look like S3 method dispatch (e.g. print.MyClass) are exempted automatically.

enumDescriptions: Require lowercase words separated by underscores (e.g. my_variable)
Require lowercase first letter, capitalized subsequent words (e.g. myVariable)
Require lowercase words separated by dots (e.g. my.variable)
Require uppercase words separated by underscores (e.g. MY_CONSTANT)
Require a single all-lowercase word (e.g. myvar)
Disable the variable-name check (function and argument checks may still run)
raven.linting.objectNameStyleVariable.description: Required naming scheme for variables (assignments whose value is not a function definition).

enumDescriptions: Require lowercase words separated by underscores (e.g. my_arg)
Require lowercase first letter, capitalized subsequent words (e.g. myArg)
Require lowercase words separated by dots (e.g. my.arg)
Require uppercase words separated by underscores (e.g. MY_ARG)
Require a single all-lowercase word (e.g. arg)
Disable the argument-name check (function and variable checks may still run)
raven.linting.objectNameStyleArgument.description: Required naming scheme for function formal arguments.

raven.chunks.highlight.enabled.description: Highlight R code chunks in .Rmd / .qmd files (and `# %%` cells in .R files) with a background color. Disable to remove the background entirely.

raven.chunks.activeCellIndicator.description: In `.R` files using `# %%` cell mode, draw a top and bottom border around the cell containing the cursor (Jupyter-style). Disable to hide the border.

raven.chunks.codeLens.commands.markdownDescription: Ordered list of run commands shown as CodeLens buttons above every R chunk header. Set to `[]` to hide all lenses while keeping the underlying commands available from the palette and keybindings. Unknown command ids are ignored.

raven.linting.objectNameSeverity.description: Severity for the object-name lint. Set to "off" to disable the rule entirely, or set individual styles to "any" to disable specific symbol kinds.

raven.linting.infixSpacesSeverity.description: Severity for the infix-spaces lint. Flags missing spaces around binary operators (`+`, `==`, `<-`, `|>`, `%>%`, etc.) and stray whitespace around tight-binding operators (`::`, `$`, `:`, unary `-/+/!`). Alignment whitespace (multiple spaces) and line-continuations are not flagged.

raven.linting.commentedCodeSeverity.description: Severity for the commented-code lint. Flags standalone comment blocks whose body parses as R code (calls, assignments, operators). Roxygen lines (`#'`), shebangs, annotation comments (`# TODO:`, `# FIXME:`, ...), and `# nolint` / `# @lsp-*` directives are never flagged.

enumDescriptions: Require double quotes ("...") for string literals.
Require single quotes ('...') for string literals.
raven.linting.stringDelimiter.description: Preferred string-literal delimiter. Used by the quotes lint. Raw strings (r"(...)") are exempt.

raven.linting.quotesSeverity.description: Severity for the quotes lint. Flags string literals not using the configured `raven.linting.stringDelimiter`. Raw strings are exempt.

raven.linting.commasSeverity.description: Severity for the commas lint. Flags whitespace before `,` and missing whitespace after `,`. A newline after `,` is fine.

raven.linting.tAndFSymbolSeverity.description: Severity for the T/F-symbol lint. Flags bare `T` / `F` identifiers used as references to `TRUE` / `FALSE`. Assignment targets, named arguments, formal parameters, and `$`/`@` field names are exempt.

raven.linting.semicolonSeverity.description: Severity for the semicolon lint. Flags `;` statement separators in source (outside strings and comments).

raven.linting.equalsNaSeverity.description: Severity for the equals-NA lint. Flags `x == NA`, `x != NA` (and the typed `NA_integer_`, `NA_real_`, `NA_character_`, `NA_complex_` variants). Use `is.na(x)` instead.

raven.linting.objectLength.description: Maximum allowed identifier length for the object-length lint. The leading `.` of hidden-identifier names is not counted; backtick-quoted and non-ASCII names are exempt.

raven.linting.objectLengthSeverity.description: Severity for the object-length lint. Flags assignment targets and formal parameters whose names exceed `raven.linting.objectLength` characters.

raven.linting.vectorLogicSeverity.description: Severity for the vector-logic lint. Flags `&` or `|` in `if` / `while` conditions (where `&&` / `||` is the scalar form). Function-call boundaries stop the scan, so `if (any(x & y))` is left alone.

enumDescriptions: Report space between `function` and `(` as errors
Report space between `function` and `(` as warnings
Report space between `function` and `(` as information
Report space between `function` and `(` as hints
raven.linting.functionLeftParenthesesSev.description: Severity for the function-left-parentheses lint. Flags whitespace between `function` (or `\`) and the parameter `(`.

enumDescriptions: Report whitespace inside brackets as errors
Report whitespace inside brackets as warnings
Report whitespace inside brackets as information
Report whitespace inside brackets as hints
raven.linting.spacesInsideSeverity.description: Severity for the spaces-inside lint. Flags whitespace immediately inside `(`, `[`, `[[` and their closing counterparts (e.g. `f( x )`, `df[ 1 ]`). Empty groupings and multi-line wrapping are left alone.

raven.linting.indentationUnit.markdownDescription: Number of spaces per indent level used by the indentation lint. Set to `"auto"` (default) to track each file's `editor.tabSize`; set to a fixed integer (e.g. `2` or `4`) to use the same unit for all R files. Mirrors `lintr::indentation_linter()`'s `indent` parameter.

raven.linting.indentationSeverity.description: Severity for the indentation lint. Flags lines whose leading whitespace doesn't match the expected indent given their AST scope (braced blocks, multi-line argument lists, continuation lines).

scripts.vscode:prepublish: bun run bundle && bun run copy-binary
scripts.pretest: bun run compile:test && bun run bundle && bun run copy-binary
scripts.package: bun run bundle && bun run copy-binary && vsce package --allow-missing-repository
scripts.package:target: bun run bundle && bun run copy-binary && vsce package --target
scripts.typecheck: tsc --noEmit && tsc --noEmit -p src/plot/webview && tsc --noEmit -p src/help/webview && tsc --noEmit -p src/data-viewer/webview
